Section 7 (Conclusions and discussions), page 24 (Question 7.1); see also Appendix A discussion around pages 28–29.

Question 7.1. When (A.8) is infeasible, what is a computationally convenient certificate for unboundedness of (A.1)?
title: Certificate for unboundedness of polynomial optimization when moment-ray condition fails
background_latex:
Let \(K\subseteq \mathbb{R}^n\) be a basic closed semialgebraic set
\[
K:=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n: c_i(x)=0\ (i\in E),\ c_j(x)\ge 0\ (j\in I)\},
\]
where \(E,I\) are finite index sets and each \(c_i,c_j\in\mathbb{R}[x]\) is a polynomial. Let \(g\in\mathbb{R}[x]\) be a polynomial of degree \(d\), and consider the polynomial optimization problem
\[
\inf\{g(x): x\in K\}.
\]
Write \(\tilde x:=(x_0,x)\in\mathbb{R}^{n+1}\). The homogenization of a polynomial \(p\in\mathbb{R}[x]\) of degree \(\deg(p)\) is
\(\tilde p(\tilde x):=x_0^{\deg(p)}p(x/x_0)\), and its highest-degree homogeneous part is \(p_{\mathrm{hom}}(x):=\tilde p(0,x)\).
Define the compact “homogenized” feasible set
\[
\tilde K:=\{\tilde x:\ \tilde c_i(\tilde x)=0\ (i\in E),\ \tilde c_j(\tilde x)\ge 0\ (j\in I),\ \|\tilde x\|_2^2=1,\ x_0\ge 0\},
\]
and the “horizon” (the section \(x_0=0\))
\[
K^{\circ}:=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n: c_{i,\mathrm{hom}}(x)=0\ (i\in E),\ c_{j,\mathrm{hom}}(x)\ge 0\ (j\in I),\ \|x\|_2^2=1\}.
\]
A (truncated) moment sequence \(z\) of degree \(d\) supported on \(K^{\circ}\) is an element of the moment cone \(R_d(K^{\circ})\), i.e., there exists a Borel measure \(\mu\) supported on \(K^{\circ}\) such that \(z_\alpha=\int x^\alpha\,d\mu\) for all \(|\alpha|\le d\). For a polynomial \(p\in\mathbb{R}[x]_d\), the pairing is \(\langle p,z\rangle:=\sum_{|\alpha|\le d} p_\alpha z_\alpha\).
The paper discusses a sufficient “decreasing-ray” moment certificate for unboundedness: feasibility of a linear moment system of the form
\[
\langle g_{\mathrm{hom}}, z\rangle=-1,\quad z\in R_d(K^{\circ}).
\]
(Equivalently, the existence of a ray in an appropriate dual moment program.) This condition is sufficient for \(\inf_{x\in K} g(x)=-\infty\), but the paper exhibits examples where \(\inf_{x\in K} g(x)=-\infty\) while this moment system is infeasible.
problem_definition_latex:
Given polynomials \(g\in\mathbb{R}[x]\) and \(c_i,c_j\in\mathbb{R}[x]\) defining \(K\subseteq\mathbb{R}^n\) as above, design a computationally convenient certificate (a verifiable condition) that implies
\[
\inf\{g(x):x\in K\}=-\infty,
\]
in the regime where the moment-ray condition
\[
\langle g_{\mathrm{hom}}, z\rangle=-1,\quad z\in R_d(K^{\circ})
\]
is infeasible.

Equivalently: characterize or certify unboundedness of \(\inf_{x\in K} g(x)\) by an efficiently checkable condition that applies even when no feasible \(z\in R_d(K^{\circ})\) satisfies \(\langle g_{\mathrm{hom}}, z\rangle=-1\).
latex:
Let \(K\subseteq \mathbb{R}^n\) be a basic closed semialgebraic set
\[
K:=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n: c_i(x)=0\ (i\in E),\ c_j(x)\ge 0\ (j\in I)\},
\]
where \(E,I\) are finite index sets and each \(c_i,c_j\in\mathbb{R}[x]\) is a polynomial. Let \(g\in\mathbb{R}[x]\) be a polynomial of degree \(d\), and consider the polynomial optimization problem
\[

\]

Define the compact “homogenized” feasible set
\[

\]

\]

Given polynomials \(g\in\mathbb{R}[x]\) and \(c_i,c_j\in\mathbb{R}[x]\) defining \(K\subseteq\mathbb{R}^n\) as above, design a computationally convenient certificate (a verifiable condition) that implies
\[
\inf\{g(x):x\in K\}=-\infty,

\[
\langle g_{\mathrm{hom}}, z\rangle=-1,\quad z\in R_d(K^{\circ})

location_in_paper: Section 7 (Conclusions and discussions), page 24 (Question 7.1); see also Appendix A discussion around pages 28–29.
open_problem_quote: Question 7.1. When (A.8) is infeasible, what is a computationally convenient certificate for unboundedness of (A.1)?
counterexample_suitability_rationale: It is feasible to search computationally for instances where a proposed certificate fails or is incomplete, because unboundedness can often be numerically suggested by finding descent rays/sequences and checking SOS/moment relaxations. However, the space of semialgebraic sets and polynomials is vast, and verifying true unboundedness (as opposed to numerical evidence) is nontrivial.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: An evolutionary coding loop could explore candidate certificate templates (e.g., alternative homogenizations, SOS/Positivstellensatz forms, auxiliary variables) and test them on automatically generated benchmark families using SDP relaxations as a fitness proxy. But turning empirical success into a general, correct certificate likely requires substantial theoretical insight beyond what automated search can ensure.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
The source paper (Nie–Yang, 2023) proposes a sufficient unboundedness certificate for \(\inf_{x\in K} g(x)=-\infty\) based on homogenization and a decreasing-ray condition on the horizon section \(K^\circ\): feasibility of \(\langle g_{\mathrm{hom}},z\rangle=-1\) with \(z\in R_d(K^\circ)\). Appendix A shows how this arises as a decreasing ray of the dual of a homogenized conic program and how it can be searched for via a hierarchy of SDP relaxations over moment/localizing matrices on the compact set \(K^\circ\). However, the paper also gives explicit examples where \(\inf_K g=-\infty\) while the horizon moment-ray system is infeasible (e.g., when \(g_{\mathrm{hom}}\ge 0\) on \(K^\circ\) but lower-degree terms drive \(g\to-\infty\) along feasible rays), motivating the open problem.

Among the forward citations provided, none resolves this gap by giving an SDP-style alternative certificate that remains valid when the horizon moment-ray condition fails. The closest conceptual alternative is the tangency/optimality-at-infinity approach of “Optimality Conditions at Infinity in Semialgebraic Vector Optimization”, which characterizes boundedness from below via finitely many asymptotic limits \(\lambda_k\) along components of a tangency variety defined by conditions of the form \(0\in \nabla g(x)+N(x;K)+\mu x\). This suggests a different computational route: certify \(\inf_K g=-\infty\) by exhibiting a tangency-at-infinity branch along which \(g\to-\infty\), potentially reducible to solving polynomial systems/critical point methods at infinity. The other citations contribute tools for handling noncompactness in SOS hierarchies or alternative liftings (e.g., adding redundant constraints like \(c-f\) to enforce bounded sublevel sets, or recession-cone-based tensor/copolytope duality), but they do not provide a general, efficiently checkable unboundedness certificate beyond the horizon moment-ray feasibility.

Overall, the problem remains open: current moment/SOS certificates based purely on \(g_{\mathrm{hom}}\) over \(K^\circ\) are not necessary for unboundedness, and forward-citing work has not yet produced a replacement certificate with comparable computational convenience. Promising directions include (a) certificates that incorporate lower-degree terms through multi-scale homogenization or weighted projective compactifications, and (b) tangency-variety/critical-at-infinity characterizations translated into polynomial-algebraic conditions amenable to SDP or Gröbner/critical-point computation.

sources.title: Optimality Conditions at Infinity in Semialgebraic Vector Optimization
sources.theorem_or_result: For minimizing a lower semicontinuous semi-algebraic function \(f\) over an unbounded closed semi-algebraic set \(S\), assuming the qualification condition at infinity \((QC)_\infty\), the paper characterizes boundedness from below and the optimal value via the tangency variety \(\Gamma(f,S)=\{x\in \mathrm{dom}f\cap S: \exists\mu\in\mathbb R\text{ with }0\in \partial f(x)+N(x;S)+\mu x\}\). Decomposing \(\Gamma(f,S)\setminus B_R\) into finitely many unbounded connected components \(\Gamma_k\), defining \(f_k(t):=f(x)\) for \(x\in \Gamma_k\cap S_t\) and \(\lambda_k:=\lim_{t\to\infty} f_k(t)\in\mathbb R\cup\{\pm\infty\}\), they prove: \(f\) is bounded below on \(S\) iff \(\min_k \lambda_k>-\infty\) (Theorem 4.1), and under \((QC)\), \[\inf_{x\in S} f(x)=\min\Big\{\min_{x\in\Sigma(f,S)} f(x),\ \min_k \lambda_k\Big\},\] where \(\Sigma(f,S)=\{x:0\in\partial f(x)+N(x;S)\}\) (Theorem 4.3).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for a computationally convenient certificate of unboundedness \(\inf_{x\in K} g(x)=-\infty\) for polynomial optimization when a specific moment-ray certificate on the horizon set \(K^\circ\) is infeasible. This paper does not address moment/SOS certificates, homogenization, or truncated moment cones. However, it provides an alternative geometric/variational characterization of when a semi-algebraic optimization problem is unbounded below: unboundedness corresponds to existence of a tangency component \(\Gamma_k\) along which \(f_k(t)\to -\infty\) (i.e., some \(\lambda_k=-\infty\)). For polynomial \(g\) and basic closed semi-algebraic \(K\), one can take \(f=g\), \(S=K\) (with \((QC)_\infty\) typically holding since polynomials are locally Lipschitz) and obtain a non-moment-based certificate: detect a tangency-at-infinity path satisfying \(0\in \nabla g(x)+N(x;K)+\mu x\) with \(g(x)\to -\infty\). The paper claims these conditions are ‘verifiable’ and gives a ‘computable formula’, but it does not provide an explicit SDP-style certificate comparable to the requested moment-ray alternative.

sources.title: Convergences of Lasserre hierarchy of an SDP relaxation for robust non-convex polynomial optimization
sources.theorem_or_result: For a convex polynomial program \(\min\{f(x): g_i(x)\le 0,\ i=1,\dots,m\}\) over a possibly noncompact semialgebraic set, the paper proves: (i) asymptotic convergence of a modified Lasserre SDP hierarchy built from the truncated quadratic module generated by \(c-f\) and \(-g_i\) (Theorem 2.1: \(f_k^*\uparrow f^*\)); (ii) if the Lagrangian \(L(x,\lambda)=f(x)+\sum_i\lambda_i g_i(x)\) has a saddle point \((x^*,\lambda^*)\) with \(\nabla^2_{xx}L(x^*,\lambda^*)\succ 0\), then \(f-f(x^*)\in M(-g_1,\dots,-g_m,c-f)\) (Theorem 3.1), implying finite convergence of the hierarchy (Theorem 3.3).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for a computational certificate of unboundedness \(\inf_{x\in K} g(x)=-\infty\) when a specific homogenized “moment-ray” condition on the horizon set \(K^\circ\) is infeasible. This citing paper does not study unboundedness certificates or horizon/moment-ray infeasibility. Instead it addresses the opposite regime: convex polynomial programs that are bounded below (so \(\inf> -\infty\)) and establishes SOS/quadratic-module representations and convergence of Lasserre-type SDP relaxations without compactness by adding the redundant constraint \(c-f\) into the quadratic module. While not a direct answer, its technique suggests a general SDP-verifiable approach to handle noncompactness by enforcing bounded sublevel sets (via \(c-f\)) and coercivity; however it does not provide a certificate that \(\inf=-\infty\) when such boundedness fails, nor does it characterize unboundedness beyond the convex/bounded-below setting.

sources.title: Merit function as a tool for vector polynomial optimization over an LMI constraint
sources.theorem_or_result:
For POPs with linear inequality constraints of the form \(F=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n:Bx\le b,\ x\ge 0\}\), the paper gives an exact lifting to a convex tensor program and then an equivalent completely positive tensor program (CPTP). Concretely, for a homogeneous objective \(f(x)=\langle A_f^h,M_d(x)\rangle\), under attainment it proves equivalence between \(\min\{f(x):x\in F\}\) and
- \(\min\ \langle A_f^h,X\rangle\) s.t. \(X\in \mathcal{C}^*_{n+1,d},\ \langle X,M_d(a)\rangle=1,\ X\times_1A\times_2A\cdots\times_dA\in \mathcal{C}^*_{m+n+1,d}\) (Theorem 5),
and similarly for inhomogeneous \(f\) an exact CPTP in \(\mathcal{C}^*_{n+2,d}\) with additional linear/tensor constraints (Theorem 6). It also derives dual copositive tensor programs and shows strict feasibility/strong duality for these primal–dual pairs (Section 4.3).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for a computationally convenient certificate of unboundedness \(\inf_{x\in K} g(x)=-\infty\) when a specific moment-ray condition on the horizon set \(K^\circ\) is infeasible. This paper does not address that moment-ray failure regime or propose an alternative unboundedness certificate based on homogenization/moments on \(K^\circ\). Instead, it develops exact completely-positive/copolytope tensor reformulations for POPs with linear inequality feasible sets and analyzes recession cones to build lifted convex models. While recession-cone analysis is conceptually related to detecting directions of unbounded descent, the paper’s main theorems assume attainment of an optimal solution and focus on equivalence/strong duality of CPTP formulations, not on certifying \(-\infty\) optimal value. Thus it provides potentially relevant tools (recession-cone-based lifting; copositive duals) but no direct certificate for unboundedness beyond the moment-ray condition in the open problem.

sources.title: Moment and polynomial optimization
sources.theorem_or_result: The paper solves an infinite-dimensional moment problem for nonnegative measures supported on the unit ball of a Sobolev space (equivalently, a compact ellipsoid of Fourier coefficients) and constructs semidefinite outer/inner approximations of the corresponding moment cone. In particular, for an index set A it defines spectrahedral outer approximations \(C^{\mathrm{out}}_{r,\rho}(A)\) via positivity on a truncated quadratic module \(Q_{r,\rho}\), proves \(C^{\mathrm{out}}_{\infty,\infty}(A)=C(A)\) (and similarly \(C^{\mathrm{inn}}_{\infty,\infty}(A)=C(A)\)), and derives a Hausdorff-distance bound \(d_H(C(A),C^{\mathrm{out}}_{r,\rho_A}(A))\le 9(2\rho_A+1)^n\, r_A^2/r^2\).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for a computational certificate of unboundedness for a finite-dimensional polynomial optimization \(\inf\{g(x):x\in K\}\) when a specific “moment-ray” certificate on the horizon set \(K^\circ\) is infeasible. This citing paper does not address unboundedness certificates for finite-dimensional POPs or propose an alternative to the failing condition \(\langle g_{\mathrm{hom}},z\rangle=-1,\ z\in R_d(K^\circ)\). Instead, it develops moment/SOS machinery for measures on compact sets in infinite-dimensional Sobolev/Fourier coefficient spaces, including semidefinite approximations of moment cones and Positivstellensatz-based representation results. These techniques are broadly related to moment-SOS methods, but they do not directly yield a new unboundedness certificate in the regime described by the open problem.
